changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_policy_captures_user_identity to k8s_audit_policy_capture_user_identity for consistency in verb tense
review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to audit logging, which is a key feature of the API server. The naming conventions mostly follow the established pattern, but a minor change was made for consistency in verb tense. The functions accurately reflect the need to enable audit logging, capture user identity, and configure log retention, aligning with the NIST control requirements.
naming_improvements: Ensure verb tense consistency in function names for clarity
title: Content Of Audit Records - Identity of any individuals, subjects,...
step1_reasoning: The control AU-3-f focuses on ensuring that audit records capture the identity of individuals or subjects involved in actions. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to the audit logging capabilities of the API server. The Kubernetes audit logging system can be configured to capture detailed information about API requests, including user identity. This aligns with the requirement to log the identity of individuals. Additionally, ensuring that audit logs are retained and properly configured supports compliance with this control.
